events.title: Triangle Pop-Up at Raleigh Iron Works
events.summary: Monthly outdoor market at Raleigh Iron Works spotlighting a rotating roster of local makers, crafters, and artisans alongside live music. Free to browse and a low-key way to discover the Triangle's creative community.

events.title: Live After 5 Free Concert Series – Opening Night
events.summary: Free Thursday-evening outdoor concerts running six weeks from May through August at Raleigh's most popular public spaces, including Moore Square and Raleigh Union Station. Bring a blanket; food and drinks are available on-site.

events.title: Got to Be NC Festival
events.summary: Free three-day showcase of North Carolina agriculture, food, music, and culture running Friday through Sunday at the State Fairgrounds. No admission charge, with hands-on activities and vendors suitable for all ages.

events.title: CackalackyCon 2026
events.summary: Weekend-long nonprofit hacker conference at RTP featuring security talks, capture-the-flag competitions, and hands-on workshops. Open to skill levels from beginner to expert, and a solid entry point into the Triangle's cybersecurity scene.

events.title: AMA Triangle May Happy Hour
events.summary: Monthly mixer hosted by the American Marketing Association's Triangle chapter and open to marketing and business professionals of all levels. A casual brewery setting makes it easy for newcomers to start building a local professional network.

events.title: The Market at NCMA
events.summary: Artisan market held on the beautiful grounds of the NC Museum of Art, pairing local makers with access to the museum's galleries and outdoor art trails. The market itself is free to browse.

events.title: AMA Triangle June Happy Hour
events.summary: Monthly professional happy hour run by the Triangle AMA chapter, set in one of Raleigh's most popular craft breweries. Informal and welcoming—ideal for transplants who want to quickly expand their professional circle.

events.title: Durham Parks Summer Concert Series – Kickoff
events.summary: Free outdoor concerts every 2nd and 4th Saturday of the month from June through August, with food trucks on-site and a rotating lineup of musical acts across Durham's public parks. A relaxed, family-friendly way to explore different Durham neighborhoods.
